pattern: Grade-school multiplication on digit arrays sidesteps big-integer types by accumulating partial products into a fixed-size buffer indexed by digit position.
hints: The product of an m-digit and an n-digit number has at most m+n digits, so reserve that many slots.
Digit i of the first number times digit j of the second contributes to positions i+j and i+j+1 of the result.
Accumulate every pairwise digit product into a result buffer of length m+n, propagate carries afterward, then strip leading zeros to form the string.

edge_inputs.description: any factor of zero yields zero, not a padded string
zero times a large number stays zero
single digits at the top of the range

stress.description: two random multi-digit numbers without leading zeros to stress carry propagation
stress.note: leading digit forced nonzero so inputs respect the no-leading-zero constraint
